questions.q: An angle of $67^{\circ}$ corresponds to $a$. Angle $x$ is adjacent to $a$ on a straight line. Find $x$.

options.why: Transfers the corresponding angle but omits the straight-line step.
Subtracts from $90$ degrees instead of a straight line.
Subtracts from a full turn instead of a straight line.

questions.q: A $124^{\circ}$ angle is vertically opposite $a$, and $a$ is alternate to $x$ on parallel lines. Find $x$.

options.why: Treats the vertically opposite or alternate pair as supplementary.
Subtracts from a full turn instead of using the two equalities.
Halves the known angle instead of transferring it unchanged.

questions.q: An angle of $132^{\circ}$ corresponds to $a$. Angle $x$ is co-interior with $a$. Find $x$.

options.why: Transfers the angle but treats co-interior angles as equal.
Uses a $360$ degree total for a co-interior pair.
Subtracts the transferred angle from $174$ instead of $180$ degrees.

questions.q: Two transferred angles of $38^{\circ}$ and $71^{\circ}$ sit adjacent to $x$ on a straight line. Find $x$.

options.why: Adds the transferred angles but does not subtract from $180$ degrees.
Subtracts the transferred angles from a full turn.
Subtracts the larger transferred angle from the smaller result.

questions.q: An auxiliary parallel through $P$ transfers angles of $34^{\circ}$ and $52^{\circ}$ to opposite sides at $P$. Find the smaller angle between the rays.

options.why: Subtracts the transferred angles instead of combining adjacent parts.
Subtracts the two angles from $180$ instead of adding them.
Finds the reflex angle instead of the requested smaller angle.
